title: Doubt-Driven Reviewer Output
description: Typed output contract for the fresh-context adversarial reviewer invoked in Step 3 (DOUBT) of the doubt-driven-development skill. Replaces freeform prose so RECONCILE, STOP, doubt-theater detection, and benchmarking become deterministic. The reviewer fills `class_hint`; the orchestrator assigns the binding class during RECONCILE — the reviewer suggests, the orchestrator decides.

status.description: Overall verdict. `clean` = no issues after thorough examination. `issues_found` = at least one finding. `cannot_assess` = reviewer lacked enough of the artifact/contract to judge (drives a recovery path, NOT a pass).

summary.description: One-line verdict. Not a substitute for findings; a human-readable headline.

findings.description: Zero or more issues. MUST be empty when status is `clean`; MUST be non-empty when status is `issues_found`.

next_actions.description: Reviewer-suggested follow-ups for the orchestrator. Advisory — the orchestrator owns the decision per RECONCILE precedence.

cannot_assess_reason.description: Required when status is `cannot_assess`. Root-cause hint feeding the recovery table (e.g. 'artifact truncated', 'contract omits the concurrency model').

id.description: Stable identifier within this review (e.g. 'F1'). Used to key the cross-cycle findings ledger so cycle N carries only open findings forward, and to set-diff single-model vs cross-model output.

title.description: Short imperative description of the issue.

class_hint.description: Reviewer's guess at the issue category. NON-BINDING. The orchestrator assigns the real RECONCILE class (contract-misread / actionable / trade-off / noise). Enumerated so it can be counted; the argument lives in `evidence`.

severity.description: Reviewer's severity. Drives the STOP condition: a cycle is 'trivial' (eligible to stop) when every finding is `minor`.

location.description: Where in the artifact (e.g. 'cache.go:142', or a region label for prose/decision artifacts). Optional — some findings are about an absence.

evidence.description: Free-text. WHY this fails the contract. Deliberately unconstrained so the adversarial reviewer can roam — structure the metadata, not the argument.

suggested_probe.description: What would confirm or refute this finding (a test, an experiment, a question for the user). Turns issues-only output into actionable output.
